methodology: SIZE-BASED estimate; per-issue batching with inline lens inference counts

ARBITER.reason: No inter-lens contradictions detected across 8 issues

vs_baseline.note: v0.3.5 costs more than A1 baseline in absolute terms. The class-split routing (haiku for TRIVIAL) saves ~$0.06 vs. running all 48 inferences at sonnet rate ($0.298 equivalent), but REVIEWER sonnet token counts (1800 input vs. A1's assumed lower-cost profile) dominate. B14 compression saves 13% on TRIVIAL input (3000 vs 3450 tokens/issue). Net effect: routing saves money on trivial work; cost rise vs A1 is attributable to higher REVIEWER inference depth in v0.3.5.

savings_analysis.description: Class-split routing (haiku for 3 TRIVIAL lenses) saves ~20% vs. running all 6 lenses at sonnet rates

notes: ARBITER not fired: 0 contradictions across 48 lens inferences on 8 issues
B13 cache-aware: stable system+briefs prefix amortized across per-issue tail — not reflected in size-based estimate per A1 methodology parity
B15 tool-subset: TRIVIAL/REVIEWER lenses restricted to read-only tools; no edit/write tool overhead
